steps.title: Notice the changing companion
steps.content: Morris's story keeps changing who else stayed well: the captain, the second mate, another man, or no one. The one constant is that Morris presents himself as the person who was not ill.

steps.title: Reject unsupported readings
steps.content: Choice A invents worry about the current captain. Choice B invents friends asking and a first trip. Choice D says there was one particular passenger, but the companion keeps changing. The main point is Morris's shifting boast. The answer is C.
